message:
perf(ft-kernel-cpu): pairwise summation in softmax / log_softmax

Continues the pairwise-summation arc started in commits 69fce3d
(sum/mean), cfde2de (norm), 56a4a6f (var/std), and 8ba4154 (sum_dim).
The softmax / log_softmax kernels already had the max-subtraction
trick for numerical stability — `exp(x - max(x))` and the standard
log-sum-exp formulation — but the inner accumulator that sums those
exponentials was naive sequential, with O(N · ε) error.

For typical classifier or attention shapes — `[B, V] softmax(dim=-1)`
where V is the vocab dim and can be 32k-256k — that drift is
visible: a 100k-element softmax row's sum departs from 1.0 by the
naive bound on every batch element. Pairwise tightens that to
O(log N · ε) so each row sums to within ~5 ULPs of 1.0.

Same fast/general two-tier pattern as the sum_dim fix:

  * Fast path (inner_size == 1, the common shape): exp(x - max)
    directly into the output slice (no scratch), then pairwise-sum
    that contiguous range, then normalise in place.
  * General strided path (inner_size > 1): gather strided values
    into a reusable scratch buffer, exp(x - max) in place there,
    pairwise-sum, then scatter the normalised values back to the
    strided output positions.

For log_softmax we don't materialise the exp values — we just need
their sum, so `pairwise_sum_map_f64(slice, |x| (x - max).exp())`
gives us log_sum_exp = max + ln(sum_exp) directly without a
materialised intermediate.

F32 siblings updated symmetrically. F32's 24-bit mantissa makes the
precision win even larger here than f64.

Tests:
  * `softmax_dim_pairwise_sums_exactly_to_one_at_large_n` — runs a
    softmax over 4 batches × 100k vocab, asserts each row sum is
    within 5e-15 of 1.0.
  * `log_softmax_dim_pairwise_logsumexp_at_large_n` — runs a
    log_softmax over 100k elements, asserts that exp(log_softmax)
    sums to 1.0 within 5e-15 (the inverse precision contract).

Quality gates: 12-crate workspace test sweep — ft-kernel-cpu 311 +
ft-api 1091 + ft-conformance 101 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-nn 150 +
ft-optim 74 + ft-data 103 + ft-serialize 15 + ft-runtime 2 +
ft-core 70 + ft-dispatch 9 + ft-device 6 — all green; clippy + ubs
clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
